examples: The ship slipped away in the darkness

meanings.definition: insert inconspicuously or quickly or quietly
examples: He slipped some money into the waiter's hand

meanings.definition: move obliquely or sideways, usually in an uncontrolled manner
examples: the wheels skidded against the sidewalk
water slipped from the polished marble

meanings.definition: to make a mistake or be incorrect

examples: He slipped me the key when nobody was looking

meanings.definition: cause to move with a smooth or sliding motion
examples: he slipped the bolt into place
the artificial hip joint luxated and had to be put back surgically

meanings.definition: moving as on a slippery surface
examples: his slipping and slithering progress over the ice
